page: Amazon Web Services m8gd.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by custom 64-core AWS Graviton processors built on the ARM64 architecture. It features 64 dedicated physical cores operating at 2.8 GHz, with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance includes 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier capabilities in memory bandwidth, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 7392.89 GB/sec and uncached large block read speeds at 464.3 GB/sec. Multi-core performance is strong, as demonstrated by a Geekbench multi-core score of 25674.0 and a Passmark database operations score of 47460.99, both placing in the top 10% of tested systems. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Passmark CPU scores falling into the average category.

The instance shows divergent performance in LLM inference tasks; while text generation for medium (7B) models ranks in the top tier at 44.6 tokens/sec, text generation for small (135M) models is relatively weak at 65.46 tokens/sec. Given its high memory bandwidth, local NVMe storage, and strong multi-threaded capabilities, this instance is suited for database operations, Redis deployments, software compilation, and static web serving. The ARMv9 architecture offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for multi-threaded workloads compared to traditional x86 architectures, though applications must be compatible with the ARM instruction set.
description: The Amazon Web Services m8gd.16xlarge is an ARM64-based general-purpose instance powered by a 64-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 256.0 GB of memory, 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a 30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench multi-core score of 25674.0 and 134.49 million Redis SET operations per second. While single-core performance and small-model LLM text generation are average to weak, its multi-threaded capabilities and high-speed local storage make it highly efficient for database operations, software compilation, and static web serving. The ARMv9 architecture provides high resource density, offering a cost-effective alternative to traditional x86 hardware for compatible workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gd.16xlarge is a 64-core ARM64 instance featuring 256 GB RAM, 3800 GB NVMe SSD storage, and top-tier memory bandwidth, optimized for databases and multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8gd.16xlarge is an ARM64 instance with 64 cores, 256 GB RAM, and 3800 GB NVMe storage, optimized for database and web serving workloads.
tagline: An ARM64-based instance combining dedicated 64-core processing, high-speed local NVMe storage, and top-tier memory bandwidth for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARM64 physical cores utilizing AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 architecture
256.0 GB of system memory paired with 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-threaded database performance
30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Suited for Redis deployments, software compilation, and static web serving
